medical_history.chief_complaint: dry and non-itching skin lesions on the face and extremities
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Symptoms started ten months ago after a fever episode. Past medical history included recurrent upper respiratory tract infections including otitis media and one episode of hospitalization for bronchopneumonia.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Recurrent upper respiratory tract infections, otitis media, bronchopneumonia.

physical_examination.general_examination: Weight of 10 kg (3-10%), height of 80 cm (3-10%), head circumference of 46 cm (10%). Pink to dark red infiltrated papules and plaques with occasional centrally oriented scales located on bilateral malar cheeks and arms. Cervical, 1x1 cm in size, non-fixing lymphadenopathy. Tachypneic and crepitant crackles present in both lung fields.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Low absolute lymphocyte number, CD4+ and CD19+ lymphocyte counts. Memory phenotype CD4+CD45RO+ T cells represented 82% of circulating CD4+ T cells and CD4+CD45RA+ cells were 5%.
Low IgA and IgE levels; normal levels of IgG and IgM.
Lymphocyte activations with PHA and ConA were ¼ of the control.
Anti-Rubella IgG >400 IU/mL during IVIg replacement therapy.
ANA was 1/320 (+), anti-dsDNA, AMA, anti-LKM, ASMA, anticardiolipin IgM/IgG, anti-TPO, antithyroglobulin were negative.

other_tests.findings: Non-Langerhans cell histiocytosis originated from dendritic cells, thought to be developed secondary to a cutaneous histiocytic reaction in the setting of immunodeficiency. Periodic acid-Schiff and Ziehl-Neelsen staining did not show any pathogen and PCR assays for mycobacteria were negative.
Homozygous missense mutation in PRKDC gene causing an amino acid change in position 3061 (c. 9239T>G; L3061R(rs587777685)).
